sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: SV Werder Bremen have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, displaying resilience with late goals. 1. FC Köln, on the other hand, have struggled recently, securing just 1 win in their last 5, with 4 losses, conceding multiple goals in most games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Werder Bremen have had the upper hand, winning both matches (2-1 at home and 1-0 away). Historical data suggests tight contests, with both teams often scoring, as seen in 6 of their last 7 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Werder Bremen (9th, 15 points) and Köln (10th, 14 points) are closely matched in the Bundesliga standings. Bremen have scored 15 goals but conceded 20, while Köln have netted 20 but also let in 19, indicating defensive vulnerabilities on both sides.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Bremen, Marco Grüll (2 goals in last 5) and Romano Schmid (key creator with 8 big chances created) are in form. Köln’s Jakub Kamiński (5 goals this season, 2 in last 5) poses a significant threat in attack.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction (Home 33%, Draw 25%, Away 42%), Köln are slight favorites, but Bremen’s home advantage at Weserstadion and recent head-to-head dominance could play a role. Expect a competitive game with goals likely on both ends due to defensive frailties.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, SV Werder Bremen lead 1-0 against 1. FC Köln at the Weserstadion. The home side secured their advantage with a well-taken header by Marco Friedl in the 22nd minute. Bremen have dominated possession and created more entries into the final third and penalty area, showing greater intent in attack. Meanwhile, Köln have struggled to convert their limited opportunities, missing a big chance in the first half. The away side will need to find a way to break through Bremen's defensive structure if they hope to turn this game around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum
halftimeSections.text: Looking at the attacking momentum, SV Werder Bremen have shown consistent periods of dominance throughout the first half. They started strongly, maintaining pressure with several attacking spells early on. There was a brief shift where 1. FC Köln gained some traction with a sustained period of attacking intent around the middle of the half. However, Bremen regained control, particularly around the time of their goal, with multiple waves of pressure that kept Köln on the back foot. Towards the end of the half, while Köln had a fleeting moment of threat, Bremen finished with a strong attacking push.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: For SV Werder Bremen, Marco Friedl has been the standout performer, not only scoring the crucial goal but also contributing defensively with interceptions and tackles. Jens Stage also played a pivotal role with an assist and creating a big chance for his team. Romano Schmid has been instrumental in midfield, dictating play with key passes. On the other side, 1. FC Köln's Eric Martel has shown some promise with a big chance created, while Said El Mala missed a significant opportunity that could have leveled the score. Köln's attacking output has been limited, and they’ll need more from their forward players to challenge in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: SV Werder Bremen have effectively controlled the game through superior ball possession and frequent entries into dangerous areas. Their ability to win possession in the final third, albeit limited, has allowed them to maintain pressure on Köln's defense. Defensively, they've been relatively solid, limiting Köln to minimal penalty area entries. On the other hand, 1. FC Köln have shown resilience in duels, particularly in the air, but their lack of shots on target and low number of final third entries highlight a struggle to build meaningful attacks. They’ve also picked up a yellow card, which could affect their approach in the second half if they push for an equalizer.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Heading into the second half, SV Werder Bremen will likely look to build on their lead by maintaining their dominance in possession and creating more big chances. With players like Friedl and Stage in form, they have the potential to add to their tally. 1. FC Köln, however, must take risks to get back into the game, which could open up spaces for Bremen to exploit on the counter. Köln will need to capitalize on any set-piece opportunities or defensive lapses from the home side. If they can muster a more aggressive attacking approach, they might find a way to level the score, but their first-half performance suggests they’ll struggle to break through.
